_comment: Zambian Bantu Phonetic Normalisation Rules — Nathan Sachitembe 2022090446

apostrophe_tonal_markers._description: Apostrophes in Bantu names mark tonal or click sounds. They are frequently omitted by data entry clerks.

bantu_prefix_normalisation._description: Bantu name prefixes are often added or dropped inconsistently across district registration offices. Normalise by stripping prefix to root form.
_examples: Mwale vs Wale — Mu+wale vs root
Kalumba vs Lumba — Ka+lumba vs root

consonant_equivalences._description: Dialect-driven consonant substitutions across Bemba, Nyanja, Tonga, Luvale. Map all variants to canonical form.
_examples: Bwalya vs Bvalya — Bemba consonant shift

vowel_normalisation._description: Long vowels written as double are often reduced to single in rushed data entry.

common_spelling_variants._description: High-frequency Zambian name spelling divergences documented from NRC registration error patterns.
_examples: Phiri vs Firi — common Nyanja surname

name_inversion_pairs._description: First and last name transposition is common when clerks record in different order.
name_inversion_pairs._note: This is handled at the record level in demographic_scorer.py, not here. Listed for documentation.
